description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.
Peru, officially the Republic of Peru, is a country in western South America.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.
Callao (Spanish pronunciation: [kaˈʝa.o]) is a seaside city and constitutional province in Peru.

description: capital and largest city of Peru
description_wiki: Lima is the capital and largest city of Peru, as well as a primate city.

description: capital city of Fukuoka prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Fukuoka (福岡市, Fukuoka-shi; [ɸɯ̥.kɯꜜ.o.ka, -kɯ.o.kaꜜ.ɕi] ) is a designated city in the Kyushu region of Japan and the capital city of Fukuoka Prefecture.

description: mountain range running along the western side of South America
description_wiki: The Andes ( AN-deez), Andes Mountains or Andean Mountain Range (Spanish: Cordillera de los Andes; Quechua: Anti) are the longest continental mountain range in the world, forming a continuous highland along the western edge of South America.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: city in and county seat of Hood River County, Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Hood River is a city in and the county seat of Hood River County, Oregon, United States.

description: seat of Multnomah County, and largest city in state of Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Portland ( PORT-lənd) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Oregon.

description: river in North America; flows from British Columbia, through Washington, and along the Washington-Oregon border to the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Columbia River (Upper Chinook: Wimahl or Wimal; Sahaptin: Nch’i-Wàna or Nchi wana; Sinixt dialect swah'netk'qhu) is the largest river in the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: infantry regiment of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 442nd Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

surface_forms: Tule Lake became a segregated camp
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

The Heart Mountain War Relocation Center, named after nearby Heart Mountain and located midway between the northwest Wyoming towns of Cody and Powell, was one of ten concentration camps used for the internment of Japanese Americans evicted during World War II from their local communities (including their homes, businesses, and college residencies) in the West Coast Exclusion Zone by the executive order of President Franklin Roosevelt (after the bombing of Pearl Harbor in December 1941, upon the recommendation of Lieutenant General John L. DeWitt).

surface_forms: people of Portland are being sent to the fairgrounds
description_wiki: The James A Banks Sr Portland Exposition Building, also known as The Expo, is a sports and convention center building complex in Portland, Maine.

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.
